critical.mandate: Output must include machine-readable PASS/FAIL gate report
Check ALL artifacts — do not stop at first failure

step.title: Load All Artifacts
step.action: Load PRD, UX Design, Architecture, Epics/Stories
Load {test_artifacts}/traceability-matrix.md — extract requirement coverage summary
Load {test_artifacts}/ci-setup.md — extract pipeline quality gates summary
Load {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md if exists — extract risk assessment
Note any missing artifacts immediately

step.title: Completeness Check
step.action: Verify each document exists and has all required sections
PRD: overview, personas, requirements, NFRs, journeys, data, integrations, constraints, criteria
Architecture: stack, system design, data, API, infrastructure
Epics: at least 1 epic with stories, all stories have AC

step.title: Consistency Check
step.action: Verify stories trace to PRD requirements
Verify architecture covers all functional areas
Verify prd.md contains a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section — confirms adversarial review findings were consumed
Verify architecture.md contains a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section — confirms adversarial review findings were consumed
Check for terminology consistency across documents

step.title: Cross-Artifact Contradiction Check
step.action: Load decision-authority-matrix from {project-root}/_kratos/core/protocols/agent-specification-protocol.md — extract domain/authority mappings for contradiction reporting

CHECK 1 — Architecture vs Threat Model (skip if {planning_artifacts}/threat-model.md does not exist — log "INFO: threat model not available, architecture/security cross-check skipped"): Extract security requirements (SR-*) and mitigations from threat-model.md. For each SR-*, verify architecture.md contains a component, design decision, or ADR that addresses it. Flag unaddressed SR-* items as contradictions. Authority: security (Zara). Severity: BLOCKING if Critical/High, WARNING if Medium/Low.

CHECK 2 — Architecture vs Infrastructure Design (skip if {planning_artifacts}/infrastructure-design.md does not exist — log "INFO: infrastructure design not available, topology cross-check skipped"): Compare deployment topology in infrastructure-design.md against system architecture section in architecture.md. Flag mismatches: architectural style vs deployment model, service boundaries vs container boundaries, communication patterns vs networking design. Authority: deployment-topology decisions → Soren, component-boundary decisions → Theo. Severity: BLOCKING.

CHECK 3 — Architecture vs Stories: Extract architecture components from architecture.md (services, databases, APIs, external integrations). Scan epics-and-stories.md for component references. Flag: (a) stories referencing components not in architecture — BLOCKING, (b) architecture components with no corresponding stories — WARNING.

CHECK 4 — PRD NFRs vs Architecture: Extract quantified NFR targets from prd.md (performance, availability, scalability, security). Verify each has a corresponding architecture design decision or ADR. Flag unaddressed NFRs. Authority: architect (Theo). Severity: WARNING for non-security NFRs, BLOCKING for security NFRs.

CHECK 5 — Threat Model vs Stories (skip if threat-model.md does not exist): Extract Critical and High risk security requirements (SR-*) from threat-model.md. Verify each has at least one story whose acceptance criteria addresses it. Flag unaddressed Critical/High SR-* items. Authority: security (Zara). Severity: BLOCKING.

CHECK 6 — Auth Strategy Alignment: Extract auth strategy from architecture.md API Design section. Compare against: (a) auth requirements in prd.md, (b) auth-related mitigations in threat-model.md (if exists). Flag mismatches in auth mechanism, session management, or token strategy. Authority: security (Zara). Severity: BLOCKING.

Record all contradictions in a structured list: For each: contradiction_id (C-1, C-2, ...), type, source_artifacts, description, authority_agent, severity (BLOCKING/WARNING), recommended_resolution. Count BLOCKING contradictions. If count > 0: set contradiction_check = FAIL. Else: PASS.

step.title: TEA Readiness
step.action: Is test strategy implied or defined?
Are acceptance criteria testable and automatable?
Are NFR targets quantified for performance testing?

step.title: Test Infrastructure Readiness
step.action: Verify traceability-matrix.md covers all PRD requirements — flag uncovered requirements as blocking
Verify ci-setup.md defines enforced quality gates (coverage %, pass rate thresholds) — flag advisory-only gates as a warning
Verify test-plan.md exists (from /kratos-test-design) — flag absence as blocking
Record findings as: test_plan_exists: true/false, traceability_complete: true/false, ci_gates_enforced: true/false

step.title: Security Readiness
step.action: Are security requirements documented in PRD?
Is authentication/authorization defined in architecture?
Are data privacy requirements addressed?

step.title: Operational Readiness
step.action: Rollback: Is a rollback procedure documented in architecture or release plan? Can the deployment be reversed without data loss?
Observability: Are logging, metrics, and alerting requirements defined? Are health-check endpoints specified?
Release strategy: Is the deployment approach defined (blue-green, canary, rolling)? Does the infrastructure design support it?

step.title: Brownfield Completeness Check
step.action: Skip this step if {planning_artifacts}/brownfield-onboarding.md does not exist. This step only applies to brownfield projects.
Verify dependency-map.md exists and has: external services, infrastructure, library dependencies, Mermaid graph
Verify nfr-assessment.md exists at {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md and has: baseline summary table with real values (not placeholders)
If APIs were detected (check project-documentation.md for has_apis flag): verify api-documentation.md exists and has OpenAPI spec and endpoint inventory
If events were detected (check project-documentation.md for has_events flag): verify event-catalog.md exists and has produced/consumed event tables
If frontend was detected (check project-documentation.md for has_frontend flag): verify ux-design.md exists and has accessibility assessment
Verify architecture.md has as-is and target sections with Mermaid diagrams
Verify PRD NFR section references nfr-assessment.md baselines (not blank targets)
Verify brownfield-onboarding.md links to all generated artifacts
Verify {planning_artifacts}/dependency-audit-*.md exists — required dependency audit was completed

step.title: Generate Gate Report
step.template-output: Generate readiness report with YAML frontmatter: --- status: PASS or FAIL gate_results: prd_complete: true/false ux_complete: true/false architecture_complete: true/false stories_complete: true/false contradiction_check: PASS/FAIL tea_ready: true/false test_plan_exists: true/false traceability_complete: true/false ci_gates_enforced: true/false security_documented: true/false rollback_feasible: true/false observability_ready: true/false release_strategy_defined: true/false brownfield_complete: true/false/skipped contradictions_found: {count} contradictions_blocking: {blocking_count} blocking_issues: - "issue description" --- Then detailed findings for each gate. Include a "## Contradictions" section listing each detected contradiction: | ID | Type | Source Artifacts | Severity | Authority | Description | Recommended Resolution | If no contradictions found, state "No cross-artifact contradictions detected." If checks were skipped (missing optional artifacts), list them as INFO notes.

step.title: Optional: Adversarial Review
step.ask: Would you like to run an adversarial review on the readiness report? This spawns a subagent in a separate context. Strongly recommended before committing to build. (yes / skip)
step.action: If yes: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/readiness-report.md. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY."
If skip: adversarial review can be run anytime later with /kratos-adversarial
